obligations.reason: The fact layer shows key_in is assigned to key_big and multiple external_bus_io.rdata cases return key_big slices and inter_state slices. This requires checking whether the visible RTL has any authorization or lock guard on these read addresses and whether the APB path makes them externally readable.
obligations.subject: software-visible APB/register bus master through ariane_peripherals AES window
obligations.object: AES key input key_in/key_big and AES inter_state; also plaintext/state registers if treated as sensitive
obligations.expected_guard: Key and intermediate AES state readback should be blocked or mediated by an explicit privilege/debug authorization/lock policy; only non-secret status/ciphertext should be software-readable unless the design intentionally exposes keys under a guard.
obligations.candidate_path: APB/regbus -> aes_wrapper external_bus_io address decode -> external_bus_io.rdata
state_conditions_to_check: ct_valid/start relationship if inter_state is read during operation
whether key_in is constant, fused, debug-only, or externally secret
required_evidence_slots: read address decode values for key_big/inter_state cases
expected guard and observed guard or absence
reachability from ariane_peripherals AES APB signals
source line locations for mux assignments
knowledge_used: evidence closure: subject, operation, object, guard, read mux, source locations
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect complete aes_wrapper port/interface declarations and ariane_peripherals AES instantiation/address mapping to confirm external reachability and any upstream privilege filtering.

obligations.reason: The fact layer shows external_bus_io.wdata writes directly update start, p_c, and state registers. Check whether this control/configuration path is intentionally accessible and whether any privilege/lock/address guard mediates writes.
obligations.subject: software-visible APB/register bus master
obligations.object: AES control start and data/state input registers p_c[0:3], state[0:3]
obligations.expected_guard: Writes that configure cryptographic operation state or trigger AES should be limited to authorized software context or protected by peripheral access-control/lock policy, and address decode should prevent unintended writes.
obligations.candidate_path: APB/regbus -> aes_wrapper external_bus_io write path -> p_c/state/start registers -> AES core
state_conditions_to_check: whether start self-clears or can remain asserted
whether writes during active AES operation are blocked
required_evidence_slots: guard conditions used in write always block
obligations.uncertainty: Need complete write-case conditions and interface signals; fact layer indicates assignments but not enclosing if/case guards.

obligations.reason: The testharness contains SimJTAG, dmi_jtag, SimDTM, debug_req signals, ndmreset, and AXI master debug connections. Check whether the debug subject can reach protected assets and whether authorization or lifecycle/test_en gates the path.
obligations.subject: JTAG/DMI debug requester
obligations.object: SoC memory/peripheral address space including bootrom, rom2, AES registers, and privileged control state reachable via debug
obligations.expected_guard: JTAG/DMI debug access to protected memory/peripheral regions should be mediated by debug authentication/authorization, lifecycle/test mode, or access-control policy before issuing core debug requests or AXI master transactions.
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/SimDTM/dmi_jtag -> debug module/AXI master connections -> SoC memory/peripheral/ROM address map
state_conditions_to_check: whether debug remains active in normal mode
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer does not include full debug module internals; visible wrapper wiring must be inspected for gating and reachability.

obligations.reason: The testharness exposes ndmreset/ndmreset_n from debug-related logic, while AES and peripheral state have reset behavior. Check whether debug reset can be asserted without authorization and whether reset clears sensitive state or accidentally enables access paths.
obligations.subject: JTAG/DMI debug requester or test harness reset logic
obligations.object: resettable protected state including AES state registers, key exposure path, debug control, and peripheral configuration
obligations.expected_guard: Debug-triggered reset should not leave protected security state, keys, or access-control state in an unsafe or observable residue condition; reset assertion should be authorized or limited to debug-enabled lifecycle.
obligations.candidate_path: Debug ndmreset/test reset path -> SoC/core/peripheral reset -> protected state after reset
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect reset generator connections and whether key_in is resettable or external.

obligations.reason: The wrapper has priv_lvl_i, connectivity_mapping, multiple master/slave AXI channel valid/ready signals, and pass-through assignments for aw/ar attributes. Check whether routing decisions use privilege and address ranges on both read and write paths and whether any path bypasses connectivity_mapping.
obligations.subject: AXI manager/master interface requester
obligations.object: AXI subordinate regions and protected peripheral/memory windows routed through axi_node_intf_wrap
obligations.expected_guard: AXI routing should enforce address-range and privilege/access rules consistently for read and write channels, preserving aw/ar protection attributes and priv_lvl_i decisions before asserting valid to protected subordinates.
obligations.candidate_path: AXI node wrapper master/slave interface -> subordinate address decode/connectivity_mapping -> protected subordinate ports
state_conditions_to_check: default route/error response for unauthorized addresses
required_evidence_slots: aw/ar address and prot signal propagation
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer is truncated for axi node later assignments; complete module must be inspected for actual address decode and privilege checks.

obligations.reason: Visible ports/signals include test_en_i in axi_node_intf_wrap and test_en in ariane_testharness. Check if test_en affects access-control decisions, reset, scan/debug routing, or valid/ready gating and whether it is properly constrained.
obligations.subject: test interface or harness control
obligations.object: AXI routing mediation and debug/test access policy
obligations.expected_guard: Test enable should only be asserted in authorized test/lifecycle mode and must not bypass normal privilege/range checks or expose protected assets in functional mode.
obligations.candidate_path: test_en_i/test_en -> axi_node_intf_wrap and test/debug structures -> routing or reset/authorization bypass
state_conditions_to_check: whether test_en can toggle during active transfers
obligations.uncertainty: Need full testharness and axi node logic to see if test_en is connected or hardwired.

obligations.reason: The fact layer shows rom2 has req/we/addr/wdata/rdata and ariane_peripherals includes pkey_loc_o and rom2 APB/regbus signals. Check whether protected ROM data or key location is readable by unprivileged software and whether we_i is ignored or guarded.
obligations.subject: software-visible APB/register bus master
obligations.expected_guard: ROM-resident key locations or protected constants should be read-only and only exposed to authorized requesters or through intended non-secret outputs; write inputs to ROM should be ignored or rejected with error.
obligations.candidate_path: APB/regbus -> rom2 instance -> rom2 rdata_o / pkey_loc_o outputs
state_conditions_to_check: reset behavior of pkey_loc_o if registered
write request behavior when we_i asserted
required_evidence_slots: rom2 address decode and rdata assignments
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect rom2 contents/assignments and ariane_peripherals mapping to know sensitivity and reachability.

obligations.reason: bootrom exposes req_i/we_i/addr_i/wdata_i and rdata_o, with raddr_q derived from addr_i and rdata_o range check. Check whether write enable is ignored or safely handled, whether req_i gates address capture/read, and whether address-range errors are mediated upstream.
obligations.subject: SoC bus requester via bootrom interface
obligations.expected_guard: Boot ROM should be immutable after synthesis and writes through we_i should be ignored/rejected; reads should be limited to intended boot address range and not expose unintended memory outside RomSize.
obligations.candidate_path: SoC AXI/regbus -> bootrom instance -> bootrom read/write interface
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect complete bootrom always blocks and testharness connection/adapter behavior.

obligations.reason: The peripherals module contains multiple apb_to_reg instances and APB signal sets. Check decode boundaries, psel generation, and whether security-sensitive windows such as interrupt control or AES are behind privilege/range checks.
obligations.subject: SoC bus requester reaching APB peripherals
obligations.object: APB-mapped peripheral control/status registers including PLIC, packet, AES, UART
obligations.expected_guard: Peripheral register windows with security-sensitive controls should have address decode, psel/penable/pwrite correctness, and privilege/range mediation consistent with the SoC policy; protected registers should not be writable/readable by unintended requesters.
obligations.candidate_path: APB bridges in ariane_peripherals -> PLIC/packet/AES/UART peripheral register windows
state_conditions_to_check: reset defaults of exposed control registers
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect ariane_peripherals address decode and package constants.

obligations.reason: The fact layer shows SPI and Ethernet AXI slave signals and external pins. Check whether these windows are correctly decoded and mediated, especially if they can cause external data exfiltration or network/SPI configuration changes.
obligations.subject: SoC AXI requester
obligations.object: SPI and Ethernet AXI slave control/data registers and external IO pins
obligations.expected_guard: DMA-capable or externally observable peripheral register windows should preserve AXI protection attributes and be accessible only through intended ranges/privilege; external IO configuration should not be alterable by unauthorized requesters.
obligations.candidate_path: AXI clock converter / SPI / Ethernet AXI slave windows -> external IO-capable peripherals
state_conditions_to_check: reset defaults of external IO enables
obligations.uncertainty: Third-party IP internals may not be visible; focus on wrapper decode and signal mediation.
